title: Introduction to Node.js
subtitle: Test your knowledge on Node.js fundamentals, architecture, core modules, and basic server implementation.

questions.t: Who developed Node.js and in what year was it released?
Which of the following best describes the architecture of Node.js?
Which built-in Node.js module allows you to create an HTTP server, make HTTP requests, and handle HTTP responses?
Which core module is used to deal with file input/output (I/O) operations in Node.js?
How does Node.js overcome its single-threaded limitation to support multi-threading?
choices: Using the cluster module to create child processes
By default, Node.js is multi-threaded and does not have this limitation
questions.a: Using the cluster module to create child processes

questions.t: Which method of the http.ServerResponse object is used to set the status code and headers before sending the response body?
Node.js uses JavaScript as a single programming language across both front-end and back-end development.
The command 'npm init' is used to execute a JavaScript file using the Node.js runtime.
In Node.js, res.end() must be called to signal the end of the response and complete the network handshake.
Streams in Node.js are objects that let you read data or write data continuously.
What command is used to initialize a new Node.js project and create a package.json file?
Which command in the terminal or command prompt is used to execute a JavaScript file named server.js with the Node.js runtime?
Which global object in Node.js specifies the name of the directory that contains the code of the application?
Which built-in Node.js module is used to handle streams that contain only binary data?
What HTTP status code is used in res.writeHead(200, ...) to indicate that the request has been successfully processed?
